mechanisms.name: M1_moving_throat
mechanisms.description: Moving throat transport: T = iσ_y has eigenvalues ±i, so each pass through a moving non-orientable throat absorbs a spectral phase ±π/2. For k passes per generation, Δ(k) = m · k · (π/2) with the eigenvalue choice encoded by m.

mechanisms.name: M2_hopf_fibre_loop
mechanisms.description: Hopf fibre loop phase: holonomy on a fibre at hyper-latitude χ is π·cos(χ). For m windings per generation, Δ(k) = m · k · π · cos(χ).

mechanisms.name: M3_antipodal_out_and_back
mechanisms.description: Antipodal out-and-back closure: a worldline traverses a closed great-circle loop of length 2π and accumulates the SU(2) geodesic phase α · 2π. For k loops per generation, Δ(k) = α · k · 2π. With α = 1/2 (spin-½) this gives k · π.

mechanisms.name: M4_worldline_crossings
mechanisms.description: Worldline crossing phase: each antipodal crossing event absorbs phase π/2 from T = iσ_y (eigenvalue arg). For k crossings of generation k, Δ(k) = m · k · (π/2). This is structurally identical to M1 — kept here as a separate entry because the physical mechanism (TX firing) is distinct even though the formula coincides.
mechanisms.delta_k_formula: Δ(k) = m · k · (π/2)   [same family as M1]

mechanisms.name: M5_antipodal_pair_with_hopf
mechanisms.description: Composite: spin-½ antipodal pass plus a Hopf fibre loop. Δ(k) = m · k · (π/2) + n · π · cos(χ). The Hopf part is k-independent (universal shift) and so cannot tighten the spread beyond what M1 alone achieves; included here as a control against accidental over-improvement claims.
mechanisms.delta_k_formula: Δ(k) = m · k · (π/2) + n · π · cos(χ)
